name: cracked-not-broken
description: Kevin Hines' 'Cracked, Not Broken: Surviving and Thriving After a Suicide Attempt' — a raw, hopeful memoir of bipolar disorder, the Golden Gate Bridge jump that should have killed him, and his journey from psychiatric wards to becoming a world-renowned suicide prevention speaker.
useCase: Understanding the lived experience of bipolar disorder with psychotic features, what drives someone to attempt suicide, the aftermath of a suicide attempt, the mental health care system, and how to find hope and purpose in recovery.
knownRisks: Contains graphic descriptions of suicide, self-harm, and psychiatric hospitalization
Kevin Hines is one survivor — his experience is not universal
May be triggering for those currently experiencing suicidal thoughts
Some medical details have been simplified for a general audience
